definition: AOT compiler that converts classical ML models (XGBoost, LightGBM, scikit-learn, CatBoost, ONNX) into native C99 inference code for faster serving.
examples: Compile a scikit-learn model to C for deployment.
Serve a CatBoost model with 336x faster inference.

definition: A software development kit (SDK) for creating homebrew games on the Nintendo 64 platform.
examples: Develop a Zelda-style game using libdragon.
Implement AI NPCs in a retro game.

definition: A caching mechanism that stores key/value states across decoding steps to enhance the efficiency of Large Language Model inference.
examples: Improve inference speed for AI models.

definition: A technique for fine-tuning Large Language Models that reduces the number of trainable parameters, making the process more efficient.
examples: Fine-tune a large model with fewer resources.
Apply LoRA to enhance model performance.

definition: Model Context Protocol, a framework for managing interactions between AI agents and their environments, facilitating efficient communication and task execution.
examples: Implement a multi-agent system using MCP.
Orchestrate AI tasks with context management.

definition: An AI agent framework that allows for the development and deployment of AI agents with version-controlled identities, rules, and skills.
examples: Manage agent skills within a git repository.

definition: An automated tool for detecting DLL hijacking vulnerabilities on Windows, providing tiered confidence scoring for exploitable DLLs.
examples: Scan a Windows application for DLL hijacking risks.
Use DLLHijackHunter in a pentesting workflow.
